content.claim: Hungary's 6- and 8-year gimnázium tracks are a post-1990 phenomenon, not inherited from the socialist era, with enrollment growing from 1% to 7.5% of 8th-grade cohorts during the 1990s, reflecting increasing demand from higher-status families.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Limited to enrollment trend documentation without causal mechanism analysis
Does not isolate drivers of parental demand growth
No cross-cohort longitudinal tracking of outcomes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
